steps.title: Identify the clause structure
steps.content: Before the blank: subject noun phrase with appositive (“Louisiana resident Oscar James Dunn” and its description). After the blank: direct object and time expression (“his term as lieutenant governor in 1868”).

steps.title: Apply the main verb requirement
steps.content: A complete main clause requires a finite verb in this position. All of A, B, and C are non-finite forms (infinitive and participles) that cannot serve as a main verb. Began is the simple past tense finite verb that completes the main clause and matches the time reference “in 1868.” That matches choice D — D is correct.
